keyMyths.title: The Burning Bush
keyMyths.description: While tending his father-in-law's sheep, Moses encounters God in a burning bush. God commands him to return to Egypt and free the Israelites from slavery, revealing his divine name, Yahweh.

keyMyths.title: The Plagues of Egypt
keyMyths.description: When Pharaoh refuses to release the Israelites, God sends ten devastating plagues upon Egypt, each targeting a specific aspect of Egyptian life and religion, demonstrating Yahweh's power over the Egyptian gods.

keyMyths.title: The Exodus and Parting of the Red Sea
keyMyths.description: Moses leads the Israelites out of Egypt. Pursued by Pharaoh's army, Moses parts the Red Sea, allowing the Israelites to cross safely while drowning the pursuing Egyptians.

keyMyths.title: Receiving the Ten Commandments
keyMyths.description: Moses ascends Mount Sinai, where he receives the Ten Commandments written on stone tablets directly from God. This establishes the foundational laws of the Israelite covenant.

keyMyths.title: Wandering in the Wilderness
keyMyths.description: For forty years, Moses leads the Israelites through the wilderness, facing challenges of hunger, thirst, and rebellion. He mediates between God and the people, teaching them God's laws and preparing them for entry into the Promised Land.

achievements: Freed the Israelites from slavery in Egypt
Received the Ten Commandments from God
Led the Israelites through the wilderness for forty years
Performed miracles through God's power, including providing water from a rock and manna from heaven
Established the Mosaic Law, which formed the basis of Israelite society and religion

sources.name: The Book of Exodus
sources.description: A primary source detailing Moses' birth, his encounter with God, the Exodus from Egypt, and the giving of the Law.
sources.title: The Book of Exodus

sources.name: The Book of Numbers
sources.description: Describes the Israelites' journey through the wilderness under Moses' leadership, including censuses, rebellions, and the establishment of laws.
sources.title: The Book of Numbers

sources.name: The Book of Deuteronomy
sources.description: Contains Moses' final speeches to the Israelites before his death, reiterating the Law and urging them to remain faithful to God.
sources.title: The Book of Deuteronomy

sources.name: The Book of Leviticus
sources.description: Details the laws and rituals given to Moses by God, including instructions for sacrifices, purity, and the priesthood.
sources.title: The Book of Leviticus

sources.name: The New Testament
sources.description: References Moses as a prophet and lawgiver, often contrasting his law with the grace and truth brought by Jesus Christ (e.g., Gospel of John, Acts of the Apostles).
sources.title: The New Testament

sources.name: Josephus, Antiquities of the Jews
sources.description: A historical account written by Flavius Josephus in the first century AD, providing additional details about Moses' life and the Exodus from a Jewish perspective.
sources.title: Josephus, Antiquities of the Jews
